lean_statement:
theorem thm_existence (m : ℝ) (hm : m ∈ Set.Ioo 0 1) :
    ∃ Ψ : Set (ℝ → ℝ) → Set (ℝ → ℝ),
      (∀ v : Set (ℝ → ℝ), ∃! u : Set (ℝ → ℝ),
        u 0 = v ∧ ∀ t, u t = Ψ v) ∧ ContinuousAlmostSure Ψ

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{thm:existence}
Let $m\in(0,1)$. With probability $1$, there is a map
\[
\Psi:\mathcal{X}_{m}\to\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{loc}}([0,\infty);\mathcal{X}_{m})
\]
so that for each $v\in\mathcal{X}_m$, $u=\Psi(v)$
is the unique strong solution in $\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{loc}}([0,\infty);\mathcal{X}_{m})$
to \cref{eq:uPDE-1} with~$u(0,x)=v(x)$.
The map $\Psi$ is continuous almost surely. Finally,
the semigroup
$P_{t}f(v)=\mathbb{E}[f(u(t,\cdot))]$
has the Feller property: if $f$ is a bounded continuous function
on $\mathcal{X}_{m}$, then so is $P_{t}f$ for any $t>0$.
semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Assumptions: \label{thm:existence} Let $m\in(0,1)$.; With probability $1$, there is a map \[ \Psi:\mathcal{X}_{m}\to\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{loc}}([0,\infty);\mathcal{X}_{m}) \] so that for each $v\in\mathcal{X}_m$, $u=\Psi(v)$ is the unique strong solution in $\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{loc}}([0,\infty);\mathcal{X}_{m})$ to \cref{eq:uPDE-1} with~$u(0,x)=v(x)$.; Finally, the semigroup $P_{t}f(v)=\mathbb{E}[f(u(t,\cdot))]$ has the Feller property: if $f$ is a bounded continuous function on $\mathcal{X}_{m}$, then so is $P_{t}f$ for any $t>0$. Conclusion: The map $\Psi$ is continuous almost surely.

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{thm}\label{thm:existence} Let $m\in(0,1)$. With probability $1$, there is a map \[ \Psi:\mathcal{X}_{m}\to\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{loc}}([0,\infty);\mathcal{X}_{m}) \] so that for each $v\in\mathcal{X}_m$, $u=\Psi(v)$ is the unique strong solution in $\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{loc}}([0,\infty);\mathcal{X}_{m})$ to \cref{eq:uPDE-1} with~$u(0,x)=v(x)$. The map $\Psi$ is continuous almost surely. lean_statement: theorem thm_maintheorem_classification (m : ℝ) (N : ℕ) (G : Set ℝ) (L : ℝ) (X : ℝ) (hm : m ∈ Set.Icc (1 / 2) 1) (hN : N ∈ Set.univ) (hG : G = Set.univ ∨ ∃ L_pos : L > 0, G = L • Set.range (Int.castRingHom ℝ)) (hL : L > 0) : ∀ a : Fin N → ℝ, ∃ nu_a : Measure (ℝ → Fin N → ℝ), (nu_a ∈ MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace.univ ∧ (∀ v : ℝ → Fin N → ℝ, v ~ nu_a → ∃ a_mean : Fin N → ℝ, a_mean = a ∧ ∃ v_sq : ℝ, v_sq = ∑ i, (v X i) ^ 2 ∧ v_sq < infty) ∧ (∀ j k : Fin N, j ≠ k → ∀ x : ℝ, Real.sign ((v j x) - (v k x)) = Real.sign (a j - a k)) ∧ (∀ c : ℝ, nu_a = MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun v => fun i => v i + c) nu_a) ∧ (∀ L' : ℝ, L' > 0 → nu_a ∈ MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace.univ) ∧ (∀ v : ℝ → Fin N → ℝ, v ~ nu_a → ∃ smooth : ℝ, smooth = 1 ∧ ∀ i : Fin N, Differentiable ℝ (v i)) ∧ (∀ q : ℝ, q ∈ Set.Ioo 0 1 → ∀ mu0 mu1 : Measure (ℝ → Fin N → ℝ), nu_a = (1 - q) • mu0 + q • mu1 → ∀ v : ℝ → Fin N → ℝ, v ~ mu0 → ∃ a_mean : Fin N → ℝ, a_mean = a)) ∧ ∀ nu_a' : Measure (ℝ → Fin N → ℝ), (nu_a' ∈ MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace.univ ∧ (∀ v : ℝ → Fin N → ℝ, v ~ nu_a' → ∃ a_mean : Fin N → ℝ, a_mean = a ∧ ∃ v_sq : ℝ, v_sq = ∑ i, (v X i) ^ 2 ∧ v_sq < infty) ∧ (∀ j k : Fin N, j ≠ k → ∀ x : ℝ, Real.sign ((v j x) - (v k x)) = Real.sign (a j - a k)) ∧ (∀ c : ℝ, nu_a' = MeasureTheory.Measure.map (fun v => fun i => v i + c) nu_a') ∧ (∀ L' : ℝ, L' > 0 → nu_a' ∈ MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace.univ) ∧ (∀ v : ℝ → Fin N → ℝ, v ~ nu_a' → ∃ smooth : ℝ, smooth = 1 ∧ ∀ i : Fin N, Differentiable ℝ (v i)) ∧ (∀ q : ℝ, q ∈ Set.Ioo 0 1 → ∀ mu0 mu1 : Measure (ℝ → Fin N → ℝ), nu_a' = (1 - q) • mu0 + q • mu1 → ∀ v : ℝ → Fin N → ℝ, v ~ mu0 → ∃ a_mean : Fin N → ℝ, a_mean = a)) → nu_a = nu_a'

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{thm:maintheorem-classification}Fix $m\in[1/2,1)$, $N\in\mathbb{N}$, and $G=\mathbb{R}$ or $G=L\mathbb{Z}$ for some $L>0$. The random variable $X$ is independent of all other random variables.
For each $\mathbf{a}\in\mathbb{R}^{N}$,
there exists a unique $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}\in\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}^{\mathrm{e}}(\mathcal{X}_{m}^{N})$
such that if $\mathbf{v}=(v_{1},\ldots,v_{N})\sim\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$,
then $\mathbb{E}\mathbf{v}(X) = \mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbb{E} |\mathbf{v}(X)|^2 <\infty$.
Moreover, the measures $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$ satisfy the following properties.
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin = 1.5cm, label = \textup{(P\arabic*)}, ref = (\textup{P\arabic*})]
 \item\label{enu:ordered} Order: with probability one, we have 
 \[
\hbox{ $\operatorname{sgn}((v_{j}-v_{k})(x))=\operatorname{sgn}(a_{j}-a_{k})$,
 for each $j,k\in\{1,\ldots,N\}$ and all $x$
in $\mathbb{R}$.}
\]
\item\label{enu:shearinvariant} 
Shear invariance: if $c\in\mathbb{R}$, then $\nu_{\mathbf{a}+(c,\ldots,c)}=\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{v}+(c,\ldots,c))$,
where $\mathbf{v}\sim\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$.
\item\label{enu:alsoinvariant} $G$-independence:
the measure $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$ is also an element of $\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G'}^{\mathrm{e}}(\mathcal{X}_{1/2}^{N})$
for $G'=\mathbb{R}$ and $G'=L'\mathbb{Z}$, for all $L'>0$.
\item\label{enu:smoothtempereddistn} If $\mathbf{v}\sim\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$ then with probability $1$, $\mathbf{v}$ is (spatially) smooth, and $\mathbf{v}$ and all of its derivatives grow at most polynomially at infinity.
  \item\label{enu:decomposesamemean} If $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}=(1-q)\mu_{0}+q\mu_{1}$ for some $q\in (0,1)$ and measures $\mu_{0},\mu_{1}\in\mathscr{P}_{G}(\mathcal{X}_{m}^{N})$,
    and $\mathbf{v}\sim\mu_{0}$, then $\mathbb{E}\mathbf{v}(x)=\mathbf{a}$.
  \end{enumerate}

assumptions: For each $\mathbf{a}\in\mathbb{R}^{N}$, there exists a unique $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}\in\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}^{\mathrm{e}}(\mathcal{X}_{m}^{N})$ such that if $\mathbf{v}=(v_{1},\ldots,v_{N})\sim\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$, then $\mathbb{E}\mathbf{v}(X) = \mathbf{a}$ and $\mathbb{E} |\mathbf{v}(X)|^2 <\infty$.

\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin = 1.5cm, label = \textup{(P\arabic*)}, ref = (\textup{P\arabic*})] \item\label{enu:ordered} Order: with probability one, we have \[ \hbox{ $\operatorname{sgn}((v_{j}-v_{k})(x))=\operatorname{sgn}(a_{j}-a_{k})$, for each $j,k\in\{1,\ldots,N\}$ and all $x$ in $\mathbb{R}$.} \] \item\label{enu:shearinvariant} Shear invariance: if $c\in\mathbb{R}$, then $\nu_{\mathbf{a}+(c,\ldots,c)}=\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{v}+(c,\ldots,c))$, where $\mathbf{v}\sim\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$.

\item\label{enu:alsoinvariant} $G$-independence: the measure $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$ is also an element of $\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G'}^{\mathrm{e}}(\mathcal{X}_{1/2}^{N})$ for $G'=\mathbb{R}$ and $G'=L'\mathbb{Z}$, for all $L'>0$.

\item\label{enu:decomposesamemean} If $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}=(1-q)\mu_{0}+q\mu_{1}$ for some $q\in (0,1)$ and measures $\mu_{0},\mu_{1}\in\mathscr{P}_{G}(\mathcal{X}_{m}^{N})$, and $\mathbf{v}\sim\mu_{0}$, then $\mathbb{E}\mathbf{v}(x)=\mathbf{a}$.
translation_statement_schema.claim: The random variable $X$ is independent of all other random variables. Moreover, the measures $\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$ satisfy the following properties. \item\label{enu:smoothtempereddistn} If $\mathbf{v}\sim\nu_{\mathbf{a}}$ then with probability $1$, $\mathbf{v}$ is (spatially) smooth, and $\mathbf{v}$ and all of its derivatives grow at most polynomially at infinity. \end{enumerate}

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{def:basinsofattraction}
For $\mathbf{a}\in\mathbb{R}^{N}$,
we denote by $\mathscr{B}_{\mathbf{a}}$ the set of all $\mathbf{v}\in L^\infty(\mathbb{R})^{N}$
such that for every $\varepsilon>0$, there exists an $L_\varepsilon\in(0,\infty)$ and
$\mathbf{v}_{-}^\varepsilon,\mathbf{v}_{+}^\varepsilon\in L^\infty(\mathbb{R}/L\mathbb{Z})^{N}$
so that $\mathbf{v}_{-}^\varepsilon\preceq\mathbf{v}\preceq\mathbf{v}_{+}^\varepsilon$ and
\begin{equation}\label{eq:basinofattractioncond}
\frac{1}{L_\varepsilon}\int_0^{L_\varepsilon}\mathbf{v}_+^\varepsilon(x)\,{d}x
-(\varepsilon,\ldots,\varepsilon)\preceq \mathbf{a}\preceq
\frac{1}{L_\varepsilon}\int_0^{L_\varepsilon}\mathbf{v}_-^\varepsilon(x)\,{d}x + (\varepsilon,\ldots,\varepsilon).
\end{equation}

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:stability-criterion}
Suppose that a function $\mathbf{v}\in L^\infty(\mathbb{R})^{N}$
can be written as $\mathbf{v}=\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{per}}+\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{int}}+\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{z}}$,
where $\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{per}}\in L^\infty(\mathbb{R}/L\mathbb{Z})^{N}$
for some $L\in(0,\infty)$, $\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{int}}\in(L^{1}(\mathbb{R})\cap L^\infty (\mathbb{R}))^{N}$,
and $\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{z}}\in L^\infty(\mathbb{R})^{N}$
is such that
\[
    \lim\limits _{|x|\to\infty}|\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{z}}(x)|=0.
\]
 Then $\mathbf{v}\in\mathscr{B}_{\mathbf{a}}$, where $\mathbf{a}=\displaystyle\frac{1}{L}\int_{0}^{L}\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{per}}(x)\,{d} x$.

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:thetawellposed}
Let $m\in(0,1)$. Almost surely, 
there is a map 
\[
\Phi :L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})\to\tilde{\mathcal{Z}}_{m},
\]
so that for each ${v}\in L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})$,
$\theta =\Phi(v)$ is the unique strong
solution to \cref{eq:thetajPDE} with the initial condition $\theta(0,\cdot)=v$.
The map $v\mapsto\Phi(v)|_{[0,T]\times\mathbb{R}}$ is measurable with 
respect to $\mathcal{F}_{T}$. Moreover, also almost surely, 
the map $\Phi$ is continuous and for any bounded set $A \subset L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})$ and $T>0$, 
the image~$\Phi(A)|_{[0, T] \times \mathbb{R}}$ is bounded in $\mathcal{Z}_{m, T}$ and the restriction $\Phi(A)|_{\{T\} \times \mathbb{R}}$ is compact in $\mathcal{X}_m$.

Local-in-time existence for the periodized

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop} \label{prop:weightedspacesizebound} Fix $0 < \ell < \ell' < 1$ and $A<\infty$. Then there is a constant $C=C(\ell,\ell',A)<\infty$ so that the following holds. Fix $L\in(0,\infty)$ and $v^{[L]}\in L^{\infty}(\mathbb{R}/L\mathbb{Z})$ with $\|v^{[L]}\|_{L_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})}\le A$ and suppose that $\|\psi^{[L]}\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}([0,1];\mathcal{C}_{g}^{1}(\mathbb{R}))}\le A$, where $g$ is the weight defined in \cref{lem:suppsi}. If, for some~$S\in(0,1]$, a function $\theta^{[L]}\in\mathcal{Z}_{S}^{[L]}$ solves \cref{eq:thetaLproblem}--\cref{eq:thetaLic}, then \begin{equation} \sup_{t\in[0,S]}\|\theta^{[L]}(t,\cdot)\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell'}}(\mathbb{R})}\le C.\label{eq:thetaLbound} \end{equation} \end{prop}
local_assumptions: suppose that $\|\psi^{[L]}\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}([0,1];\mathcal{C}_{g}^{1}(\mathbb{R}))}\le A$, where $g$ is the weight defined in \cref{lem:suppsi}.

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop}[$L^{1}(\mathbb{R})$-contraction] \label{prop:L1Rcontraction}Fix $m\in(0,1)$. If $\mathbf{u}=(u_{1},u_{2})\in\mathcal{Z}_{m}^{2}$ solves \cref{eq:uPDE-many}, then for all $t\ge 0$ we have \begin{equation} \|u_{1}(t,\cdot)-u_{2}(t,\cdot)\|_{L^{1}(\mathbb{R})}\le\|u_{1}(0,\cdot)-u_{2}(0,\cdot)\|_{L^{1}(\mathbb{R})}.\label{eq:L1decreasing} \end{equation} \end{prop}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop}[Conservation of mass] \label{prop:conservation}Fix $m\in(0,1)$. If $\mathbf{u}=(u_{1},u_{2})\in\mathcal{Z}_{m}^{2}$ solves \cref{eq:uPDE-many} and \begin{equation} \|u_1(0,\cdot)-u_2(0,\cdot)\|_{L^1(\mathbb{R})}<\infty,\label{eq:L1startsfinite} \end{equation} then for all $t\ge 0$ we have \begin{equation} \int_\mathbb{R}[u_1(t,x)-u_2(t,x)]\,{d}x = \int_\mathbb{R}[u_1(0,x)-u_2(0,x)]\,{d}x.\label{eq:meanpreserved} \end{equation} \end{prop}

semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Quantifiers: forall Assumptions: \label{lem:approxconvex}If $F:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}_{\geq 0}$ satisfies $F(0)=0$ and $F''=c_{1}\delta_{0}$ with some $c_{1}\ge 0$, then there is a family~$F_{\varepsilon}\in\mathcal{C}^{2}(\mathbb{R})$ of convex functions that has the following properties: \begin{enumerate} \item There is a constant $C<\infty$ so that for all $\varepsilon \in (0, 1]$ and $x \in \mathbb{R}$, we have \begin{align} & F_{\varepsilon}(x) \le C(|x| + \varepsilon),\label{eq:Fbd} \\ & |xF_{\varepsilon}'(x)| \le CF_{\varepsilon}(x),\label{eq:Fprimebd} \\ & |F_{\varepsilon}'(x)| \le C,\label{eq:Fepsprimebounded} \\ & |x|F_{\varepsilon}''(x) \le C\mathbf{1}_{[0,\varepsilon]}(x).\label{eq:Fprimeprimebd} \end{align} \item The restriction $F_{\varepsilon}|_{\mathbb{R}\setminus[-1,1]}$ is independent of $\varepsilon$. Conclusion: \item We have \begin{equation} \lim_{\varepsilon\to0}\|F_{\varepsilon}-F\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathbb{R})}=0.\label{eq:FepstoF} \end{equation} \end{enumerate}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop}[Conservation of mass in the probability space]\label{prop:L1omegaconservation} % Let~$\mathbf{u}=(u_1,u_2)\in\mathcal{Z}_m^2$ be a solution to~\cref{eq:uPDE-many} such that $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in\mathscr{P}_G(L^\infty_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}(\mathbb{R})^2)$, with some $m\in(0,1)$, and set $\eta=u_1-u_2$. Assume also that \begin{equation}\label{eq:finitemoment} \sup_{\substack{i\in\{1,2\}\\ t\in[0,T] } }\mathbb{E} \left[\int_{\Lambda_G} u_{i}(t,x)^{2} \, {d}\lambda_G(x)\right] < \infty. \end{equation} Then, for all $t \geq 0$ we have \begin{equation}\label{jun2510} \mathbb{E}\left[\int_{\Lambda_{G}}\eta(t, x)\,{d}\lambda_{G}(x)\right]=\mathbb{E}\left[\int_{\Lambda_{G}}\eta(0,x)\,{d}\lambda_{G}(x)\right]. \end{equation} % \end{prop}
definitions: Assume also that \begin{equation}\label{eq:finitemoment} \sup_{\substack{i\in\{1,2\}\\ t\in[0,T] } }\mathbb{E} \left[\int_{\Lambda_G} u_{i}(t,x)^{2} \, {d}\lambda_G(x)\right] < \infty.

\label{prop:ordering-morespecific}Let $m\in(0,1)$.
  Let $\mathbf{u}=(u_1,u_2)\in\mathcal{Z}_{1/2}^2$ solve \cref{eq:uPDE-many} with random initial conditions independent of the noise, and let $T>0$ be such that one of the
  following two conditions holds:
  \begin{enumerate}[leftmargin = 1.5cm, label = \textup{(H\arabic*$'$)}, ref = (\textup{H\arabic*$'$})]
  \item
    \label{enu:hyp-stat-morespecific}
    $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in{\mathscr{P}}_{G}(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}\infty(\mathbb{R})^{2})$
 for $G=L\mathbb{Z}$ with some $L>0$ or $G=\mathbb{R}$, \cref{jun2518} holds, and
 %
%
%
%
%
    $ \displaystyle \int_{\Lambda_G}\mathbb{E}|u_1(t,x)-u_2(t,x)|\,{d} \lambda_G(x)$ is constant on $[0, T]$.
\item
    \label{enu:hyp-L1-morespecific}
Almost surely,  $\|u_{1}(t,\cdot)-u_{2}(t,\cdot)\|_{L^{1}(\mathbb{R})}=\|u_{1}(t',\cdot)-u_{2}(t',\cdot)\|_{L^{1}(\mathbb{R})}<\infty$ for all $t,t'\in [0,T]$.
  \end{enumerate}
  Then, almost surely, $\operatorname{sgn}(u_{1}(t,x)-u_{2}(t,x))$ is a random constant independent of $x \in \mathbb{R}$ and $t \in [0, T]$.

Conclusion: \end{enumerate} Then, almost surely, $\operatorname{sgn}(u_{1}(t,x)-u_{2}(t,x))$ is a random constant independent of $x \in \mathbb{R}$ and $t \in [0, T]$.

[Krylov--Bogoliubov]
  \label{prop:krylovbogoliubov}Fix $m\in(0,1)$, $N\in\mathbb{R}$,
  and $G=L{\mathbb Z}$ with $L>0$ or $G={\mathbb R}$.  %
  If $s\ge0$, $T_{k}\uparrow\infty$,
  and $\nu_{0}\in\mathscr{P}_{G}(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty})$ are such that
  \[
    \nu\coloneqq\lim\limits _{k\to\infty}\frac{1}{T_{k}}\int_{s}^{s+T_{k}}P_{t}^{*}\nu_{0}\,{d} t
  \]
  exists in the sense of weak convergence of measures on $\mathcal{X}_{m}^{N}$,
  then $\nu\in\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}(\mathcal{X}_{m}^{N})$.

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop} \label{prop:boundedtightness} Let $\mathbf{u}\in\mathcal{Z}_{1/2}^{N}$ solve \cref{eq:uPDE-1} with bounded initial condition $\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot)\in L^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{N}$. Then the family of random variables $\{\mathbf{u}(t,\cdot)\}_{t\ge1}$ is tight with respect to the topology of $\mathcal{X}_{1/2}^{N}$, and \begin{equation} \sup_{t\ge0,x\in\mathbb{R}}\mathbb{E}|\mathbf{u}(t,x)|^{2}<\infty.\label{eq:supu2bdd} \end{equation} \end{prop}
definitions: Let $\mathbf{u}\in\mathcal{Z}_{1/2}^{N}$ solve \cref{eq:uPDE-1} with bounded initial condition $\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot)\in L^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{N}$.
local_assumptions: if the initial condition is in addition periodic in space, then the resulting subsequential limits are extremal.

\label{lem:KPZexpsubadd}
  Fix $m \in (0, 1)$ and $G=L{\mathbb Z}$ or $G=\mathbb{R}$, and
  let $X \sim \mathrm{Uniform}(\Lambda_G)$ be independent of all else.
  Let $v \in L^\infty(\mathbb{R})$ be $G$-invariant and let $\tilde{v} \sim \tilde{\nu} \in \overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}(\mathcal{X}_{m})$ satisfy $\mathbb{E} \tilde{v}(X)^2 < \infty$.
  Let $u$ and $\tilde{u}$ solve \cref{eq:uPDE-1} with initial data $v$ and $\tilde{v}$, respectively.
  Consider solutions $h$ and $\tilde{h}$ to \cref{eq:KPZ} obtained 
  from $u$ and $\tilde{u}$, respectively, as in \cref{prop:KPZsolntheory}.
  Then for all $t \geq 0$ and $x \in \mathbb{R}$ we have
  \begin{equation}\label{eq:expectationboundofh}
    \mathbb{E}[\tilde{h}(t,x)-h(t,x)]\le\sup_{x\in\mathbb{R}}[\mathbb{E}\tilde{h}(0, x) - h(0, x)].
  \end{equation}

\label{prop:onlycouplingisthesame}
 Let $m\in(0,1)$, $G=L{\mathbb Z}$ with $L>0$ or $G=\mathbb{R}$, and $\Lambda_G$ be a fundamental
  domain for~$G$. 
 Suppose that $\nu\in\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}(\mathcal{X}_{m}^{2N})$, and 
let
  \[
    \mathbf{v}=(\mathbf{v}_{1},\mathbf{v}_{2})=((v_{1,1},\ldots,v_{1,N}),(v_{2,1},\ldots,v_{2,N}))\sim\nu,
  \]
  and $\nu_{i}=\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{v}_{i})$, so that
$\nu_{1},\nu_{2}\in\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}(\mathcal{X}_{m}^{N})$,    and let $\nu_{i,j}$ be the marginal law of~$\nu_{i}$ on the $j$th
  component.
  Suppose that  for each $i=1,2$,  $j=1,\dots,N$, we have~$\nu_{i,j}\in\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}^{\mathrm{e}}(\mathcal{X}_{m})$,
  and 
\begin{equation}\label{jul604}
      \mathbb{E}\left[ \int_{\Lambda_G} v_{i, j}(x)^2 \, {d}\lambda_G(x) \right] < \infty.
    \end{equation}
If, in addition, we have
    \begin{equation}\label{jul602}
      \mathbb{E}\left[\int_{\Lambda_G}v_{1,j}(x)\,{d}\lambda_{G}(x)\right]=\mathbb{E}\left[\int_{\Lambda_G}v_{2,j}(x)\,{d}\lambda_{G}(x)\right],~~ 
      \hbox{for each $j=1,\dots,N$, }
 \end{equation}
%
%
%
%
%
%
%
then $\mathbf{v}_{1}=\mathbf{v}_{2}$ almost surely.

    (L : ℝ) (hL : L > 0) (N : ℕ) (nu1 nu2 : Measure (ℝ → ℝ))

    (hnu1_ergodic : ∀ j : Fin N, ∃ nu : Measure ℝ, nu = nu1.restrict (fun x => x j))

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:boosttheweight}
Let $\mathcal{T}$ be a metric space 
and $w_{1},w_{2},w_{3}$ be weights on $\mathbb{R}$ 
so that 
\[
\lim\limits _{|x|\to\infty}\frac{w_{1}(x)}{w_{2}(x)}=0.
\]
Suppose that $u_n\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R}))$, and $u\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}(\mathbb{R}))$ satisfy
\begin{equation*}
\lim\limits _{n\to\infty}
\|u_{n}-u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}(\mathbb{R}))}=0 \quad \textrm{and} \quad \sup\limits _{n\in\mathbb{N}}\|u_{n}\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R}))}<\infty.
\end{equation*}
Then $u\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{2}}(\mathbb{R}))$, and
 $\lim\limits _{n\to\infty}\|u_{n}-u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{2}}(\mathbb{R}))}=0$ as well.

Conclusion: \end{equation*} Then $u\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{2}}(\mathbb{R}))$, and $\lim\limits _{n\to\infty}\|u_{n}-u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}(\mathcal{T};\mathcal{C}_{w_{2}}(\mathbb{R}))}=0$ as well.

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:arzelaascoli}Suppose that $w_{1},w_{2},w_{3}$ are weights
  so that $\lim\limits _{|x|\to\infty}\frac{w_{1}(x)}{w_{2}(x)}=0$ and fix $\alpha > 0$.
  Then the embedding
  \[
    \mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})\cap\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})\hookrightarrow\mathcal{C}_{w_{2}}(\mathbb{R})
  \]
  is compact, where $\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})\cap\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})$
  is equipped with the norm $\|u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})\cap\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})}=\|u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})}+\|u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{p}_{w_{3}}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})}$.

  so that $\lim\limits _{|x|\to\infty}\frac{w_{1}(x)}{w_{2}(x)}=0$ and fix $\alpha > 0$.
  Then the embedding
  \[
    \mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})\cap\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})\hookrightarrow\mathcal{C}_{w_{2}}(\mathbb{R})
  \]
  is compact, where $\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})\cap\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})$
  is equipped with the norm $\|u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})\cap\mathcal{C}_{w_{3}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})}=\|u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{w_{1}}(\mathbb{R})}+\|u\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{p}_{w_{3}}}^{\alpha}(\mathbb{R})}$.

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lem}[Mild solutions are classical]\label{lem:mildisclassical} Fix $m\in(0,1)$, $L\in(0,\infty]$, $T>0$, and $v^{[L]}\in L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})$. If $\theta^{[L]}\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}((0,T];\mathcal{X}_{m})$ satisfies \cref{eq:mildformulation}, and we extend it to $t=0$ by $\theta^{[L]}(0,\cdot)=v^{[L]}$, then~$\theta^{[L]}\in\tilde{\mathcal{Z}}_{m,T}$ and $\theta^{[L]}$ is a classical solution to the PDE \cref{eq:thetaLproblem}. Also, for any $p\in[1,\infty)$ and any $\ell'>m+1/p$, we have \begin{equation} \theta^{[L]}\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}([0,T];L_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell'}}^{p}(\mathbb{R})).\label{eq:thetactsinLp} \end{equation} Finally, if $v^{[L]}$ is continuous, then \begin{equation} \theta^{[L]}\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}([0,T];\mathcal{X}_{m}).\label{eq:thetaLctsconverges} \end{equation} \end{lem}

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop} \label{prop:localintimeexistence}For each $L\in(0,\infty)$ and $v^{[L]}\in L^{\infty}(\mathbb{R}/L\mathbb{Z})$, there is a time $S\in(0,2]$, depending on $L$, $\psi^{[L]}$, and $v^{[L]}$, so that there exists a solution $\theta^{[L]}\in \bigcup_{S' \in (0, S)}\mathcal{Z}_{S'}^{[L]}$ to \cref{eq:thetaLproblem}--\cref{eq:thetaLic}. Moreover, if $S<2$, then \begin{equation} \limsup_{t\to S}\|\theta^{[L]}(t, \cdot)\|_{L^{\infty}(\mathbb{R}/L\mathbb{Z})}=\infty.\label{eq:limsuptoinfty} \end{equation} \end{prop}
definitions: Let us mention that one reason not to skip the proof completely is that it is there that the bound \cref{eq:time-deriv-control} is used.

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:vCauchy}Fix $\ell\in(0,1)$, $T>0$. Suppose $v_{i}\in L_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell}}^\infty(\mathbb{R})$
  and $\theta_{i},\psi_{i}\in\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}((0,T];\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell}}(\mathbb{R}))$
  satisfy
  \[
    \theta_{i}(t,\cdot)=G_{t}*v_{i}-\int_{0}^{t}\partial_{x}G_{t-s}*(\theta_{i}(s,\cdot)+\psi_{i}(s,\cdot))^{2}\,{d} s
  \]
  for all $t\in[0,T]$ and $i\in\{1,2\}$. Fix $\beta\in(2\ell\vee(3/2),2)$
  and define the weight $\mathrm{q}_{\lambda}(x)=\exp(\lambda\langle x\rangle^{\beta})$
  as in \cref{lem:hk-superexp}. Then there exists a constant $C=C(\ell,\beta,T)<\infty$
  so that
  \begin{equation}
    \|\theta_{1}-\theta_{2}\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}((0,T];\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{q}_{1+T}}(\mathbb{R}))}\le\mathrm{e}^{CX}\left(\|v_{1}-v_{2}\|_{L_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell}}^\infty(\mathbb{R})}+\|\psi_{1}-\psi_{2}\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}([0,T];\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell}}(\mathbb{R}))}\right),\label{eq:vCauchyineq}
  \end{equation}
where
\[
X=1+\sum_{i=1}^2\Big[
    \|v_i\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}((0,T];\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell}}(\mathbb{R}))}
    +\|\psi_i\|_{\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{b}}((0,T];\mathcal{C}_{\mathrm{p}_{\ell}}(\mathbb{R}))}\Big].
\]

    (hη : ContDiff ℝ 3 η) (hI : IsClosed I) (hx1 : x1 ∈ I) (hx2 : x2 ∈ I)
    (hx12 : x1 ≠ x2) (hη_eq : η x1 = η x2)
    (hη_deriv : (deriv η x1 > eps ∧ deriv η x2 > eps) ∨ (deriv η x1 < -eps ∧ deriv η x2 < -eps))
    (hA : A = (sSup {‖deriv^[k] η‖_infty | k ∈ Finset.range 4}).toReal) :
    abs (x1 - x2) ≥ Real.sqrt (2 * eps / A)

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{lem:howmanyderivs}Let $\eta\in\mathcal{C}^{3}(I)$ for some  closed interval $I \subset \mathbb{R}$ and define
  $A=\|\eta\|_{\mathcal{C}^{3}(I)}$. If $x_{1}\ne x_{2}\in I$
  satisfy $\eta(x_{1})=\eta(x_{2})$ and $\eta'(x_{1}),\eta'(x_{2})>\varepsilon$
  or $\eta'(x_{1}),\eta'(x_{2})<-\varepsilon$, then $|x_{1}-x_{2}|\ge\sqrt{2\varepsilon/A}.$

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lem} Suppose that $\zeta \geq 0$ is in the Schwartz class and $\eta$ is a smooth function all of whose derivatives have at most polynomial growth at infinity. Define \[ g(\lambda)=\sum_{\substack{y\in\eta^{-1}(\lambda)\\ \eta'(y)\ne0 } }|\eta'(y)|\zeta(y). \] Then $g$ is a continuous function of $\lambda$.\label{lem:gcts} \end{lem}
definitions: Let $\eta\in\mathcal{C}^{3}(I)$ for some closed interval $I \subset \mathbb{R}$ and define $A=\|\eta\|_{\mathcal{C}^{3}(I)}$.
assume that $x_2 > x_1$ and $\eta'(x_{1}),\eta'(x_{2})>\varepsilon$.
Let $\delta=x_{2}-x_{1}$.
Let $\zeta_{k} \geq 0$ be smooth such that $\operatorname{supp}\zeta_{k}\subset[k-1,k+2]$ and $\sum\limits_{k\in\mathbb{Z}}\zeta_{k}=\zeta$.
Let \begin{equation} g_{k}(\lambda)=\sum_{\substack{y\in\eta^{-1}(\lambda)\\ \eta'(y)\ne0 } }|\eta'(y)|\zeta_{k}(y).
Let $A_{k}=\|\eta\|_{\mathcal{C}^{3}([k-1,k+2])}+\|\zeta_{k}\|_{\mathcal{C}^{0}(\mathbb{R})}+\|\eta'\zeta_k\|_{\mathcal{C}^1(\mathbb{R})}$ + 1.
local_assumptions: Suppose that $\zeta \geq 0$ is in the Schwartz class and $\eta$ is a smooth function all of whose derivatives have at most polynomial growth at infinity.
nearby_claims: theorem and the assumption $\mathbb{E}(X_{2}-X_{1})^{-}>-\infty$ yields $\mathbb{E}(X_{1}-X_{2})\le0$, so in particular $\mathbb{E}|X_{1}-X_{2}|<\infty$.
lemma will be used in the proof of \cref{lem:gcts} below.

theorem prop_L1omega
  {alpha : Type*} [MeasurableSpace alpha] [NormedAddCommGroup alpha] [NormedSpace ℝ alpha]
  (u : ℝ → alpha → ℝ × ℝ)
  (F : ℝ → ℝ)
  (η : ℝ → alpha → ℝ)
  (L : ℝ)
  (c : ℝ)
  (hL : L > 0)
  (hc : c > 0)
  (hF : ∀ x, F x ≥ 0)
  (hF'' : ∀ x, HasDerivAt (deriv F) (c * (if x = 0 then 1 else 0)) x)
  (hη : ∀ t x, η t x = (u t x).1 - (u t x).2)
  (hA : ∃ (A : ℝ), A < infty ∧ ∀ i t, ∫ x in Set.Icc 0 L, ‖(u t x).1‖ ^ 2 ≤ A)
  (hLaw : ∃ mu, mu = (u 0 ·).measure ∧ mu ∈ Set.univ)
  : ∀ t ≥ 0,
    ∫ x in Set.Icc 0 L, F (η t x) ≤ ∫ x in Set.Icc 0 L, F (η 0 x)

semantic_equivalence_artifact.normalized_natural_language_theorem: Quantifiers: forall Assumptions: [$L^{1}$-contraction in the probability space]\label{prop:L1omega} Suppose that $\mathbf{u}=(u_{1},u_{2})\in\mathcal{Z}_{m}^{2}$, with some $m\in(0,1)$, is a solution to \cref{eq:uPDE-many} such that $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in\mathscr{P}_G(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{2})$ for $G = L\mathbb{Z}$, with some $L>0$, and that \begin{equation} A\coloneqq\sup_{\substack{i\in\{1,2\}\\ t\in[0,T] } }\mathbb{E} \left[\int_{\Lambda_G} u_{i}(t,x)^{2} \, {d}\lambda_G(x)\right] < \infty.\label{eq:L1omegamoments} \end{equation} Let $F:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ satisfy $F''=c\delta_{0}$ for some $c>0$, and set $\eta=u_{1}-u_{2}$.; Then for all $t\ge 0$ we have \begin{equation} \mathbb{E}\int_{0}^LF(\eta(t, x))\,{d}x + \frac{c}{4}\mathbb{E}\int_0^t\sum_{\substack{y\in[0,L]\\\eta(s,y)=0}} |\partial_x\eta(s,y)|\,{d}s \le \mathbb{E}\int_0^LF(\eta(0,x))\,{d}x.\label{eq:L1omega} \end{equation} Furthermore, if $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in\mathscr{P}_{\mathbb{R}}(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{2})$ then for all $T\ge 0$ and $x\in\mathbb{R}$ we have \begin{equation}\label{jun2516} \mathbb{E}F(\eta(T,x))\le\mathbb{E}F(\eta(0,x)). Conclusion: \end{equation}

theorem prop_L1omega
  {alpha : Type*} [MeasurableSpace alpha] [NormedAddCommGroup alpha] [NormedSpace ℝ alpha]
  (u : ℝ → alpha → ℝ × ℝ)
  (F : ℝ → ℝ)
  (η : ℝ → alpha → ℝ)
  (L : ℝ)
  (c : ℝ)
  (hL : L > 0)
  (hc : c > 0)
  (hF : ∀ x, F x ≥ 0)
  (hF'' : ∀ x, HasDerivAt (deriv F) (c * (if x = 0 then 1 else 0)) x)
  (hη : ∀ t x, η t x = (u t x).1 - (u t x).2)
  (hA : ∃ (A : ℝ), A < infty ∧ ∀ i t, ∫ x in Set.Icc 0 L, ‖(u t x).1‖ ^ 2 ≤ A)
  (hLaw : ∃ mu, mu = (u 0 ·).measure ∧ mu ∈ Set.univ)
  : ∀ t ≥ 0,
    ∫ x in Set.Icc 0 L, F (η t x) ≤ ∫ x in Set.Icc 0 L, F (η 0 x)
extracted_assumptions: [$L^{1}$-contraction in the probability space]\label{prop:L1omega} Suppose that $\mathbf{u}=(u_{1},u_{2})\in\mathcal{Z}_{m}^{2}$, with some $m\in(0,1)$, is a solution to \cref{eq:uPDE-many} such that $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in\mathscr{P}_G(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{2})$ for $G = L\mathbb{Z}$, with some $L>0$, and that \begin{equation} A\coloneqq\sup_{\substack{i\in\{1,2\}\\ t\in[0,T] } }\mathbb{E} \left[\int_{\Lambda_G} u_{i}(t,x)^{2} \, {d}\lambda_G(x)\right] < \infty.\label{eq:L1omegamoments} \end{equation} Let $F:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ satisfy $F''=c\delta_{0}$ for some $c>0$, and set $\eta=u_{1}-u_{2}$.

Then for all $t\ge 0$ we have \begin{equation} \mathbb{E}\int_{0}^LF(\eta(t, x))\,{d}x + \frac{c}{4}\mathbb{E}\int_0^t\sum_{\substack{y\in[0,L]\\\eta(s,y)=0}} |\partial_x\eta(s,y)|\,{d}s \le \mathbb{E}\int_0^LF(\eta(0,x))\,{d}x.\label{eq:L1omega} \end{equation} Furthermore, if $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in\mathscr{P}_{\mathbb{R}}(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{2})$ then for all $T\ge 0$ and $x\in\mathbb{R}$ we have \begin{equation}\label{jun2516} \mathbb{E}F(\eta(T,x))\le\mathbb{E}F(\eta(0,x)).

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{prop}[$L^{1}$-contraction in the probability space]\label{prop:L1omega} Suppose that $\mathbf{u}=(u_{1},u_{2})\in\mathcal{Z}_{m}^{2}$, with some $m\in(0,1)$, is a solution to \cref{eq:uPDE-many} such that $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in\mathscr{P}_G(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{2})$ for $G = L\mathbb{Z}$, with some $L>0$, and that \begin{equation} A\coloneqq\sup_{\substack{i\in\{1,2\}\\ t\in[0,T] } }\mathbb{E} \left[\int_{\Lambda_G} u_{i}(t,x)^{2} \, {d}\lambda_G(x)\right] < \infty.\label{eq:L1omegamoments} \end{equation} Let $F:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ satisfy $F''=c\delta_{0}$ for some $c>0$, and set $\eta=u_{1}-u_{2}$. Then for all $t\ge 0$ we have \begin{equation} \mathbb{E}\int_{0}^LF(\eta(t, x))\,{d}x + \frac{c}{4}\mathbb{E}\int_0^t\sum_{\substack{y\in[0,L]\\\eta(s,y)=0}} |\partial_x\eta(s,y)|\,{d}s \le \mathbb{E}\int_0^LF(\eta(0,x))\,{d}x.\label{eq:L1omega} \end{equation} Furthermore, if $\operatorname{Law}(\mathbf{u}(0,\cdot))\in\mathscr{P}_{\mathbb{R}}(L_{\mathrm{p}_{m+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})^{2})$ then for all $T\ge 0$ and $x\in\mathbb{R}$ we have \begin{equation}\label{jun2516} \mathbb{E}F(\eta(T,x))\le\mathbb{E}F(\eta(0,x)). \end{equation} \end{prop}
definitions: assume that $G=L\mathbb{Z}$ for some $L \in (0, \infty)$, so $\Lambda_{G}=[0,L)$.
Let $F:\mathbb{R}\to\mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}$ satisfy $F''=c\delta_{0}$ for some $c>0$, and set $\eta=u_{1}-u_{2}$.

definitions: Let $m\in(0,1)$.
let $T>0$ be such that one of the following two conditions holds: \begin{enumerate}[leftmargin = 1.
let $\mathbf{u}=(u_1,u_2)\in\mathcal{Z}_m^2$ and $T$ satisfy the assumptions of \cref{prop:ordering-morespecific}, including either \ref{enu:hyp-stat-morespecific} or \ref{enu:hyp-L1-morespecific}.
assume that $G=L\mathbb{Z}$ for some $L\in(0,\infty)$, since if $G=\mathbb{R}$ then \cref{enu:hyp-stat-morespecific} holds for $G=L\mathbb{Z}$ for every $L$.

\label{prop:minvariance}
Fix $m\in(0,1)$ and let $G=L\mathbb{Z}$ or $G=\mathbb{R}$. Fix
a deterministic, $G$-invariant function $v\in L^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})$
and let $\delta_{v}\in {\mathscr{P}}_{G}(\mathcal{X}_{m})$ 
be the delta measure on $v$. Suppose that~$T_{k}\to+\infty$, and 
  \begin{equation}\label{jul118}
  \frac{1}{T_k}\int_{0}^{T_{k}}P_{t}^{*}\delta_{v}\,{d}t\to \nu\in\mathscr{P}(\mathcal{X}_{m}),
  \end{equation}
weakly.

  be independent of everything else. Then we have
  \[
    \operatorname{Var} [w(X)] \le\operatorname{Var}[\tilde{w}(X)].
  \]

semantic_equivalence_artifact.original_latex_theorem:
\label{prop:limitsareergodic}
  Suppose that $G=L{\mathbb Z}$ with $L>0$ or $G={\mathbb R}$, and that a deterministic 
  function~$v\in L^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})$ is~$G$-invariant. Let $\delta_{v}$ be the measure on $L_{\mathrm{p}_{1/2+}}^{\infty}(\mathbb{R})$
  with a single atom at $v$. If~$s\ge0$,~$T_{k}\uparrow\infty$,
  and $\nu\in\mathscr{P}(\mathcal{X}_{1/2})$ are such that
  \[
    \nu = \lim_{k\to\infty}\frac{1}{T_{k}}\int_{s}^{s+T_{k}}P_{t}^{*}\delta_{\mathbf{v}}\,{d} t
  \]
  in the sense of weak convergence of probability measures on $\mathcal{X}_{1/2}$,
  then $\nu\in\overline{\mathscr{P}}_{G}^{\mathrm{e}}(\mathcal{X}_{1/2})$.
